violations.text: Various foods were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. The foods in the prep unit that were stored above the cold hold line were observed 43-45 degrees. Food was recently placed in unit and was relocated to cool down. Food in the downstairs reach-in were observed 44-53 degrees. Food was disposed of if out of temperature for more than 4 hours. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution in spray bottle was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. PIC refilled sanitizer to adequate ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Caramelized onions were placed hot in the refrigerator in covered plastic containers. Food was cooked 3.5 hours ago. Food was moved to sheet pan and placed in walk-in. Proper cooling reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings and live activity. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025
The reach-in located in the basement area and salad prep door gaskets were not in good repair. Reach-In taken out of service until serviced/replaced/repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of bio-film. Per PIC, ice machine will be cleaned and sanitized by end of day. The non-food contact surfaces of the various shelving in the basement have an accumulation rodent droppings. Areas to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025
Rodent droppings were observed in various locations in the basement. Areas to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025
An allergy statement is needed on the menu. Per PIC, allergy statement will be added soon due to menus needing to be reprinted. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

Food in the prep unit stored above the cold hold line was observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was recently placed in unit and was moved to colder refrigeration. Establishment to no longer store food above the cold hold line. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/15/2024
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 02/15/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

The floor in the various locations in the establishment has an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/15/2024
